data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Google.com employs a microservices architecture to handle different functionalities independently.
data.technical_spec: Services are designed as independent components that communicate over HTTP/2 and gRPC.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Advanced load balancing techniques are used to distribute incoming traffic efficiently.
data.technical_spec: Uses Google Cloud Load Balancing to manage traffic and ensure high availability.

data.title: Serverless Computing
data.description: Parts of Google's architecture utilize serverless computing to handle dynamically scaling events.
data.technical_spec: Uses Google Cloud Functions and Cloud Run for event-driven workloads.

data.title: Content Delivery Network
data.description: A global CDN is employed to quickly deliver content to users worldwide.
data.technical_spec: Google Cloud CDN is used to cache content at edge locations globally.

data.title: Database Management
data.description: Robust database management ensures the handling of massive data efficiently.
data.technical_spec: Cloud Spanner and Bigtable are utilized for scalable and consistent database solutions.
